title: Test plan
description: Test cases for the application, linked to the estimate through the epic id (and optionally the task id): that link is what makes it possible to measure test coverage per epic by comparing 'cases' with 'epics' in estimate.json. It is the source of truth for the functional, integration and regression tests of the project; the export (xlsx or another format) is generated by the backend from this JSON. Each case describes preconditions, ordered steps with a per-step expected outcome, an overall expected result and an execution outcome — designed to be filled in progressively: first written as a draft (id, title, epic, steps), then executed and updated with outcome/tester/run_at during acceptance or internal testing.

meta.description: Metadata of the project and of the test plan.

title.description: Readable title of the test plan (e.g. 'Test plan - Orders portal v1').

client.description: Client of the project. Null if not specified or not relevant to the test plan.

date.description: Date the test plan was generated or last updated, YYYY-MM-DD format.

status.description: Editorial state of the document, for the 'chat stays conversational' UX: 'draft' while it is still being refined with the user in chat (citing cases by id, e.g. TC3), 'confirmed' once the user has validated the set of cases. Optional field: a document without 'status' is treated as a draft.

notes.description: Free-form notes on the test plan as a whole (e.g. the test environment used, shared test data, known coverage limits). Null if not needed.

cases.description: The test cases, in presentation order. They need not follow the order of the epics in the estimate: a case may cut across several features, but it must still declare the epic it primarily verifies so that coverage can be computed.

id.description: Identifier of the test case, unique within the document, e.g. 'TC12'. Used to refer to the case in chat and in the links with the estimate.

title.description: What the case verifies, in one concise line understandable by anyone (e.g. 'Rejecting a request requires a mandatory reason').

epic.description: Id of the estimate epic (epics[].id in estimate.json, e.g. 'E2') this case verifies. It is the key field for computing test coverage per epic: every epic of the estimate should have at least one case referencing it.

task.description: Id of the estimate task (epics[].tasks[].id in estimate.json, e.g. 'E2.T3') the case is linked to more specifically than the epic, when it verifies a single, precise feature. Omitted when the case covers the epic as a whole or cuts across several tasks.

area.description: Functional area of the application the case belongs to (e.g. 'Request management', 'Spreadsheet import', 'Notifications'), useful to group or filter cases independently of the estimate epic.

type.description: Nature of the test case: 'functional' verifies an expected behaviour of the system under normal conditions; 'integration' verifies the exchange of data with external systems (APIs, files, legacy systems); 'regression' re-verifies a behaviour already tested, after a change elsewhere in the system; 'negative' verifies that the system behaves correctly when given invalid input, missing permissions or error conditions.

preconditions.description: State of the system and data required before starting the case (e.g. a user with role X already signed in, a request already in the 'In progress' state, an import file already uploaded to a share). If the case needs no particular preconditions, say so explicitly instead of leaving it empty (e.g. 'None, start from the home page').

steps.description: The ordered sequence of actions the tester must perform to execute the case, each with the outcome expected right after that step. This is the heart of the test case: it must be executable by a tester who did not write the brief, so every action must be described concretely and, when a matching mockup page exists, refer to that page or component (e.g. 'From the Requests grid, click Edit on the row of the request just created').

n.description: Ordinal number of the step within the case, starting at 1, with no gaps.

action.description: What the tester does in this step (e.g. 'Fill the Amount field with 1500 and click Save'). Refer to the mockup page or component when one exists, so the tester knows where to look without having to interpret the brief.

expected.description: What must happen right after THIS step (e.g. 'The system shows the save confirmation message'). It may be missing on pure navigation steps (e.g. 'Open the Requests list page') that produce no verifiable outcome of their own.

expected_result.description: The overall expected outcome of the case as a whole, distinct from the intermediate outcomes of the individual steps (e.g. 'The request is in the Approved state and appears in the history with the approving user and the current date').

outcome.description: Outcome of the last execution of the case: 'to_run' while it has not been executed yet (the implicit default if the field is absent), 'ok' if the observed behaviour matches the expected result, 'ko' if it does not (a defect to report), 'blocked' if the case cannot be executed because of an external cause (e.g. the environment is unavailable, a precondition record is missing).

tester.description: Name or initials of whoever executed (or is executing) the case. Empty string if not assigned yet.

run_at.description: Date of the last execution of the case, YYYY-MM-DD format. Absent while the case is 'to_run'.

notes.description: Free-form notes on the execution (e.g. the defect ticket opened if outcome='ko', an anomalous but non-blocking behaviour observed, the test data used).

brief_ref.description: Chapter or section of the brief (or of another requirements document) that defines the behaviour this case verifies, for traceability should the outcome be disputed (e.g. 'Brief 4.2 - Handling rejections').
